data.title: SEC's Plan to Reduce Financial Reporting Frequency Advances
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has moved closer to implementing a plan that would allow publicly traded companies to reduce their financial reporting frequency from quarterly to semi-annually. According to PANews, this plan has passed a White House review, marking a significant step towards its implementation. The review was completed earlier this week, enabling the SEC to officially announce the plan and seek public feedback. After receiving comments, the SEC commissioners will need to vote on the final version of the rule, which typically occurs several months later.

U.S. President Donald Trump had called for the change from quarterly to semi-annual reporting last year, prompting the SEC to pursue this reform of corporate disclosure requirements. Since 1970, U.S. publicly traded companies have been required to disclose financial information on a quarterly basis.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Rising Gold and Silver Prices Impact Copper Production Costs
data.description: Copper miners Southern Copper Corp. and Vale SA are experiencing a unique financial scenario as the prices of byproducts such as gold and silver soar. Bloomberg posted on X that this surge in byproduct prices has led to a situation where the cost of producing copper has effectively become negative. This development highlights the significant impact of precious metal market fluctuations on the copper mining industry.

data.title: Debate Over Emergency Powers in DeFi Protocols After Arbitrum and Circle Actions
data.description: Arbitrum's decision to freeze funds linked to the $293 million Kelp DAO exploit has reignited discussions about the role of emergency intervention powers in decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols. According to NS3.AI, this move has sparked debate on whether such protocols should have the authority to intervene in emergencies. Circle, the issuer of USDC, also faced scrutiny following the $280 million Drift exploit, as the company stated that USDC freezes require a lawful process and legal compulsion. Industry executives argue that while emergency controls can safeguard users, they must be transparent and established beforehand.

data.title: Shuttle Pharmaceutical Holdings to Acquire United Dogecoin in $11 Million Merger
data.description: Shuttle Pharmaceutical Holdings has announced its agreement to acquire United Dogecoin in a merger supported by $11 million in PIPE financing. According to NS3.AI, this strategic move aims to establish a public Dogecoin mining operation. United Dogecoin stated that the merger would facilitate the deployment of 3,000 ElphaPex mining rigs within 60 days of the deal's closure. These rigs are expected to generate 43,200 GH/s, representing approximately 1.5% of the current global Dogecoin mining capacity.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Storage Sector Stocks Rise with Micron Technology Leading Gains
data.description: U.S. storage sector stocks experienced an upward trend, with Micron Technology (MU.O) seeing a significant increase of 5.07%. According to Jin10, SanDisk (SNDK.O) also rose by 4.03%, while Western Digital (WDC.O) turned positive. Additionally, Seagate Technology (STX.O) climbed by 5.32%.

data.title: Solana Achieves Milestone with Over 10 Billion Transactions in a Quarter
data.description: Solana has reached a significant milestone by surpassing 10 billion transactions in a single quarter. According to NS3.AI, this marks the first time the blockchain network has achieved such a high transaction count within a three-month period. This development highlights Solana's growing adoption and the increasing activity on its platform. The surge in transactions reflects the network's scalability and efficiency, which have been key factors in its rising popularity among developers and users. As Solana continues to expand its ecosystem, this achievement underscores its potential to handle large volumes of transactions efficiently.

data.title: White House Proposes Amendments to Iran Nuclear Agreement
data.description: The White House has reportedly sent Iran proposed amendments to a draft agreement, aiming to re-include the nuclear issue. According to NS3.AI, one of the amendments, sent by U.S. envoy Witkoff on April 28, requests that Iran refrain from removing enriched uranium from a bombed nuclear facility or resuming activity there during the negotiations.

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations are poised to impact liquidation levels on major centralized exchanges. According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass indicates that if Ethereum's price falls below $2,206, the cumulative liquidation intensity for long positions across mainstream centralized exchanges could reach $874 million (8.74亿美元). Conversely, if Ethereum surpasses $2,412, the cumulative liquidation intensity for short positions could amount to $403 million (4.03亿美元).

data.title: Dollar Index Declines as Brent Crude and Silver Show Volatility
data.description: On May 1, the Dollar Index (DXY) continued its downward trend, decreasing by 0.35%. According to Jin10, Brent crude oil experienced a significant drop of 4.00% during the day, currently priced at $105.69 per barrel. Meanwhile, spot silver saw a substantial increase of 4.00%, with its current price at $76.67 per ounce.

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Prices Drop Amid Iran's New Proposal
data.description: WTI crude oil prices have experienced a sharp decline, falling from $108 to $103 per barrel over the past four hours, according to Odaily. This drop follows reports that Iran has submitted a new proposal through Pakistan, potentially shifting focus towards ending the ongoing conflict. Additionally, Axios global affairs correspondent reports that the United States has also presented a revised nuclear agreement list to Iran via a Pakistani intermediary.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Uncertainty Surrounds Inflation Path
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan has expressed concerns about the uncertain trajectory of inflation. According to Jin10, Logan highlighted the challenges in predicting inflation trends, emphasizing the need for careful monitoring and analysis. The statement reflects ongoing debates within the Federal Reserve regarding inflation management and economic stability.

data.title: UK Bonds Rise as Market Reduces Bets on 2026 Bank of England Rate Hike
data.description: UK government bonds experienced an increase, as market participants reduced their expectations for a Bank of England interest rate hike in 2026. According to Jin10, this shift in market sentiment reflects changing economic conditions and investor outlooks regarding future monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Crypto Venture Capital Funding Drops 74% in April 2026
data.description: Crypto venture capital funding experienced a significant decline in April 2026, reaching $659 million, a 74% decrease from $2.6 billion in March. According to NS3.AI, this marks the lowest monthly total since July 2024. Data from CryptoRank indicates that 63 funding rounds were completed in April, contributing to a total of $5.64 billion in funding for 2026 so far. The monthly financing scale has been on a downward trend since October 2025, coinciding with a roughly 37% decrease in the total crypto market capitalization over the same period.

data.title: Curve introduces a "bad debt" recovery mechanism, allowing affected creditors to exit through trading or participate in remediation.
data.description: Curve Finance has announced the introduction of a "bad debt" recovery mechanism based on an on-chain market mechanism. This mechanism allows CRV users who have experienced bad debts in certain lending markets to choose different recovery strategies: directly selling their claims to exit, continuing to hold and waiting for potential repairs, or providing liquidity to earn fees and incentives. The core of this mechanism is the establishment of a trading pool between crvUSD and the tokens representing the bad debts, allowing bad debt claims to be priced in the market and generate liquidity, thus providing users with an immediate exit channel, rather than relying solely on the final liquidation result. It is understood that after the crypto market crash last October, some of Curve Finance's lending markets experienced bad debt issues. Various liquidity pools were impacted by sharp price fluctuations and liquidity contractions, resulting in some depositors facing withdrawal restrictions and asset losses. Curve stated that the recovery mechanism does not eliminate losses or guarantee recovery, but rather gradually reflects risk and repair expectations through market mechanisms. Furthermore, if the governance layer distributes rewards through the veCRV incentive mechanism, it will help improve liquidity depth, improve exit conditions, and enhance market pricing efficiency.

data.title: Axios Global Affairs Reporter: The US submits a list of revisions to the nuclear agreement to Iran through Pakistani intermediaries.
data.description: Axios global affairs correspondent Barak Ravid wrote on the X platform that on Monday, White House Special Envoy Steve Witkoff sent a revised list to Iran through a Pakistani intermediary. The core demand was to reinstate the nuclear issue to the agreement text. According to sources, one of the revisions requires Iran to commit to refraining from any activity at the bombed nuclear facilities and from touching any part of its enriched uranium stockpile during the negotiations.

data.title: VIX Index Hits Lowest Level in Over Two Months
data.description: The VIX Index, a key measure of market volatility, has dropped to its lowest point in more than two months, reaching 16.72. According to ChainCatcher, this decline indicates a reduction in market fear and uncertainty, suggesting a period of relative stability in the financial markets.

data.title: Iran's New Proposal Faces Challenges in Nuclear Negotiations
data.description: Iran has introduced a new proposal, which has been routed through Pakistan, in the ongoing nuclear negotiations. According to NS3.AI, the likelihood of these negotiations succeeding appears slim. As a result, attention may pivot towards efforts to conclude the ongoing conflict.

data.title: Dogecoin Wallets Reach Record Holdings Amid Market Outperformance
data.description: Dogecoin wallets holding at least 100 million DOGE have reached a record 108.52 billion DOGE, as the token has outperformed the broader crypto market over the past month. According to NS3.AI, a descending triangle breakout setup has positioned DOGE's upside target near $0.131, approximately 20% above the current price, while $0.088 is identified as the key downside level.

data.title: The USDC Treasury minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain.
data.description: According to Whale Alert, the USDC Treasury minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana chain.

data.title: USDC Treasury Mints 250 Million USDC on Solana
data.description: Whale Alert has reported that the USDC Treasury has minted 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain. According to Odaily, this development was detected through blockchain monitoring services.
